purpose: Map the native N03/E3 four-pole packet pulse onto an N04 movement substrate through state-mediated boundary coherence coupling.

S1.interpretation: mapped to the center of the tracked basin

K2.interpretation: mapped to the front interior shoulder

S2.interpretation: mapped to the front boundary mask

K1.interpretation: mapped to the rear boundary mask

mapping_notes: This is the first explicit N04 mapping from the four-pole E3 route to a movement substrate.
The mapping is region-based, not node-id preserving, because E3 has 4 nodes while S0_chain_v1 has 21 nodes.
Boundary coupling changes node coherence only; it does not write support masks, centroids, or displacement values.

S1_ring_v1.reason: Ring mapping requires a separate wrap/unwrap and front/rear convention for pulse-boundary coupling.

coupling_signal_scope.note: K2 and S2 are mapped for route completeness and future multi-pole coupling signals, but coupling_signal_v1 uses only S1 and K1.

coupling_policy.coupling_strength_rationale: Chosen for the first fixture-validation run to produce a measurable but bounded boundary mass transfer from the E3 pulse signal without opening movement classification in Iteration 8.

application.symmetric_null: add equal coherence to front and rear boundary masks and remove the same total from the center reservoir
application.asymmetric_forward: add coherence to front boundary mask and remove the same total from rear boundary mask
application.asymmetric_reversed: add coherence to rear boundary mask and remove the same total from front boundary mask

P0_pulse_disabled_control.expected: no boundary coupling and no movement claim

P1_symmetric_boundary_coupling_null.expected: pulse-mediated boundary activity with no net directed movement claim

P2_asymmetric_boundary_coupling_forward.expected: measurable front gain and rear release, movement claim still blocked until M4/M5 classifier

P2_asymmetric_boundary_coupling_reversed.expected: reversed boundary coupling direction using the same positive E3 pulse telemetry; true reversed-E3-pulse control is deferred

true_reversed_e3_pulse_lane.reason: Iteration 8 validates the boundary-coupling fixture only. A true pulse-direction reversal should use counter-clockwise or pole-reversed E3 telemetry and be classified in the M4/M5 gate.
